Examples.Title: Generate an authentication link
Examples.Description: Creates a login link that allows end users to connect their fitness tracking account
Examples.Code:
### Generate an authentication link
# @name Authentication_AuthenticateUser
POST {{host}}/auth/authenticateUser?resource=FITBIT
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}
Content-Type: application/json
Accept: application/json

{
  "language": "string",
  "reference_id": "string",
  "auth_success_redirect_url": "string",
  "auth_failure_redirect_url": "string"
}

## Responses
# 200
# Description: Returned when authentication link could be successfully generated
# Content-Type: application/json
# 400
# Description: Returned when one or more parameters is malformed - an appropriate error message will be returned
# Content-Type: application/json
# 404
# Description: Returned when a parameter does not exist on Terra's end (e.g. resource)
# Content-Type: application/json

Examples.Title: Generates an authentication token for the Terra mobile SDKs
Examples.Description: Creates a token to be used with initConnection() functions in the Terra mobile SDKs in order to create a user record for Apple Health or Samsung Health (or equivalent)
Examples.Setup: This example assumes `using Terra;` is in scope and `apiKey` contains the required credential.

Examples.Title: Get all Terra User IDs
Examples.Description: Used to query for information for all Terra User IDs. Supports optional pagination via `page` and `per_page`. If `page` is not provided, it returns all users in one go (backwards compatibility).
Examples.Setup: This example assumes `using Terra;` is in scope and `apiKey` contains the required credential.
